title: clematis acutangula subsp. ranunculoides
intro: Clematis acutangula subsp. ranunculoides, also known as Manjishtha, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the Clematis genus. This plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Clematis acutangula subsp. ranunculoides include the treatment of skin conditions such as acne, eczema, and psoriasis, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels and improve digestion. The herbal preparations of this plant include decoctions, infusions, and powder forms, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
